name: timeline-report
tagline: Fetches a project's claude-mem timeline via localhost API + SQLite, then has a sub-agent write a long narrative history report.
verdict: Safe to install: all network calls stay on localhost (the local claude-mem worker) and the only file read is the user's own local claude-mem.db — no exfiltration, no remote code execution, no hidden instructions. Minor issues: the ROI/token-savings math bakes in unexplained arbitrary constants (30% relevance factor, ~10K tokens/recall) presented as calculate

highlights.summary: Step 5 writes to a default path `./journey-into-PROJECT_NAME.md` and will silently overwrite an existing file of the same name on rerun; not catastrophic but unconfirmed overwrite.

Step 4 passes the ENTIRE fetched timeline (built from prior session observations) verbatim as trusted context into a sub-agent; if any stored observation text contained injected instructions, the summ
No 'when NOT to use' guidance is given, e.g. for tiny projects with few observations or when the worker is known to be down; user must infer from Error Handling section instead.
